data.title: Venus Concept targets sequential Q4 growth with Venus NOVA launch amid Venus Hair sale uncertainty
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Venus Concept Inc. (VERO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Rajiv Kanishka De Silva reported 2% year-over-year growth in sales of energy-based devices (EBD), highlighting "strong execution from the team in a continued challenging environment" and a focus on "customer engagement and support as well as improving sales efficiency by prioritizing core products in the U.S., including Bliss MAX and Versa PRO."
  • De Silva acknowledged a high single-digit year-over-year decline in total revenue, attributing softness to the Venus Hair Restoration segment and ongoing "customer financing pressures, economic uncertainty and tight credit markets." He cited additional uncertainty due to the pending sale of the Venus Hair business to MHG Co. Ltd.
  • A major product milestone was announced: "On November 10, we announced 510(k) clearance for the Venus NOVA, the first product clearance from the company's new focused R&D strategy discussed on our earnings calls over the last year." De Silva described Venus NOVA as "a next-generation multi-application platform designed to redefine noninvasive treatments for the body, face and skin."
  • De Silva noted that the company is "targeting sequential growth in the fourth quarter, fueled in part by a limited commercial launch of this innovative new body and skin system in December."
  • The CEO emphasized the company's evolving product strategy, stating: "Our product portfolio will continue to evolve and deliver more than just leading device performance, shifting towards a focus on total practice performance from the moment the patient enters the clinic to post-treatment management."
  • De Silva addressed the Venus Hair sale process, revealing delays: "We have experienced challenges with our counterparty in bringing this transaction to a conclusion and have sought the assistance of the Delaware Court to aid in this respect."
  • CFO Domenic Penna stated, "We reported total revenue of $13.8 million, down $1.2 million or 8% year-over-year."
  • Penna detailed product mix, noting, "Total revenue from the sale of energy-based devices or EBD, increased 2% year-over-year to $9.5 million." He added, "Cash sales represented approximately 73% of total systems and subscription revenue in the third quarter, of which cash sales in the U.S. were 82% of U.S. systems and subscription revenue compared to 76% last year."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated their goal: "We are targeting sequential growth in the fourth quarter, fueled in part by a limited commercial launch of our Venus NOVA innovative body and skin system in December."
  • The company is not providing full year 2025 guidance due to "active dialogue with existing lenders and investors, ongoing evaluation of strategic alternatives with various interested parties to maximize shareholder value and current market conditions impacted by trade disruptions."

Financial Results

  • CFO Penna reported, "Gross profit for the third quarter of 2025 decreased $1.1 million or 11% to $8.8 million compared to the third quarter of 2024."
  • Gross margin was 64%, down from 66.1% in the prior year, attributed to "the impact of U.S. tariffs on our devices imported into the U.S. market and to a lesser extent, higher device system cost of goods sold."
  • Operating expenses increased $1.2 million or 7% to $18.3 million, with approximately one-third of the increase due to "legal and other professional fees incurred to support the sale of the Venus Hair business."
  • Operating loss was $9.5 million, and net loss attributable to stockholders was $22.6 million or $12.14 per share. Penna also noted, "Net interest and other expenses were $12.5 million," primarily driven by an $11.3 million noncash loss on debt extinguishment.
  • As of September 30, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $5.9 million and total debt obligations of approximately $30.1 million.

Q&A

  • No analyst questions were present during the call, and the operator confirmed, "We are currently showing no additional questions in the queue."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts did not participate in the Q&A, resulting in a neutral sentiment from the analyst side.
  • Management's prepared remarks conveyed confidence in stabilization of EBD sales, product innovation, and balance sheet improvements, while also acknowledging challenges. Phrases like "We are encouraged by the team's continued focus" and "We anticipate the U.S. commercial introduction of Venus NOVA to contribute to the company's long-term growth profile" reflect a slightly positive tone, tempered by cautious language regarding the Venus Hair sale and uncertain market conditions.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similar optimistic tone about product launches and strategic progress, but the current quarter included more discussion of legal and transactional delays, reflecting a slightly more cautious undertone.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Venus NOVA received 510(k) clearance in Q3 2025, with the company planning a commercial launch in December, compared to the previous quarter's expectation of a body device launch by early 2026.
  • Revenue declined to $13.8 million in Q3 from $15.7 million in Q2, with EBD sales noted as a stabilizing factor in Q3.
  • The operating loss widened in Q3 relative to Q2, and the net loss increased, partly due to a significant noncash loss on debt extinguishment.
  • Management continued to emphasize cost containment, product mix optimization, and the pending Venus Hair business sale, but described greater delays and legal challenges this quarter compared to a more straightforward transaction expectation last quarter.
  • Analyst interaction remained minimal, with no live Q&A in Q2 and no questions in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • CEO De Silva highlighted "customer financing pressures, economic uncertainty and tight credit markets" as ongoing challenges.
  • The Venus Hair business sale has faced delays, with management "seek[ing] the assistance of the Delaware Court to aid in this respect."
  • Gross margin pressure was attributed to "the impact of U.S. tariffs on our devices imported into the U.S. market."
  • Legal and professional fees increased due to the Venus Hair sale process.
  • The company is not providing full year guidance due to "current market conditions impacted by trade disruptions" and ongoing strategic evaluations.

Final Takeaway

Venus Concept delivered modest growth in energy-based devices and took a significant step with the 510(k) clearance of Venus NOVA, targeting sequential growth in the fourth quarter as the new platform launches. The company continues to navigate challenges tied to the Venus Hair business sale, macroeconomic headwinds, and tariff-related cost pressures, while improving its balance sheet through debt exchanges and capital raises. Management remains focused on strategic portfolio evolution and disciplined cost management, positioning the business for long-term growth despite short-term uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: CSCO, DIS, BTDR, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Thursday as investor optimism waned following the official end of the record-length federal government shutdown.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Playboy (PLBY) +26% - Shares surged after the company posted its third straight quarter of adjusted EBITDA growth and swung to a $500K profit (breakeven per share), surpassing expectations for a $0.02 loss. Results were weighed down by $2.5M in litigation costs, but excluding those, adjusted EBITDA would have risen to $6.6M. CEO Ben Kohn highlighted the milestone, noting, “Even with the impact of our litigation expenses, this quarter was our first since going public to report net income.”
  • Sealed Air (SEE) +19% - Shares surged after reports that private equity firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice is in talks to acquire the packaging company. The Wall Street Journal reports that a deal could finalize soon, but discussions could still collapse or attract competing bids. Sealed Air, best known for inventing Bubble Wrap, carries a market value of about $5.4B, and a transaction with a typical takeover premium would value it above that level.
  • Firefly Aerospace (FLY) +15% - Shares surged after the company reported Q3 revenue up 98% Q/Q and 38% Y/Y, surpassing expectations, though profit figures fell short. Firefly projected FY2025 revenue of $150M–$158M, above estimates of $135.48M. CEO Jason Kim credited the growth to strong execution across spacecraft and launch teams, adding that the recent SciTec acquisition strengthens the company’s national security capabilities.
  • Cisco (CSCO) +4% - Shares jumped after the tech giant posted FQ1 results above expectations and raised its full-year outlook. CEO Chuck Robbins said Cisco is on track for its “strongest year yet,” citing robust demand for secure networking and AI-driven technologies. For FQ2, the company projects revenue of $15B–$15.2B (above the consensus of $14.6B) and adjusted EPS of $1.01–$1.03 (above the consensus of $0.98). Full-year FY2026 guidance was also lifted, with EPS of $4.08–$4.14 (vs. prior $4.00–$4.06) and revenue of $60.2B–$61B (vs. prior $59B–$60B).

Biggest stock losers

  • Bitdeer (BTDR) -22% - Shares slumped after the company announced plans to offer $400M in Convertible Senior Unsecured Notes due 2031 in a private placement, with an option for initial purchasers to buy an additional $60M. The proceeds will be used for datacenter expansion, ASIC-based mining rig development, and growth in its HPC and AI cloud business, as well as for capped call transactions and note repurchases. Bitdeer also unveiled a registered direct offering of Class A shares to certain holders of its 5.25% convertible notes due 2029, with proceeds intended to repurchase those notes partially.
  • The Walt Disney Company (DIS) -8% - Shares dropped after the media giant reported mixed FQ4 results, with revenue down 0.5% Y/Y. Segment performance was uneven; Sports rose 2% and Experiences climbed 6%, but Entertainment fell 6%. CEO Robert Iger said the company made “great progress” in strengthening its business and advancing its direct-to-consumer strategy, emphasizing continued investment in high-quality content and shareholder returns. Looking ahead, Disney projects FY2026 operating cash flow of $19B, ahead of the $16.9B consensus estimate.
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR) -3% - Shares fell after Goldman Sachs double-downgraded the stock to Sell from Buy, citing limited upside potential as further improvement “gets harder.” Analyst Kate McShane noted that while long-term margin gains are possible, stronger fundamentals already seem priced in. Goldman expressed concerns about weaker trends among lower-income consumers and declining price/value perception, favoring peers such as Ollie’s Bargain ((OLLI)) and Five Below ((FIVE)) instead. The firm set a price target of $103, implying a 3% downside from Wednesday’s close.

data.title: Wall Street opens lower as post-shutdown optimism fades
data.description:
Wall Street opened lower on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite bottoming the pack, as the optimism from the government shutdown ending faded away.

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.5%, the Dow (DJI) -0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.11%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.60%.

The government shutdown was the longest in U.S. history, as it lasted 43 days.

The U.S. House of Representatives on Wednesday passed the stopgap spending bill that will end the longest U.S. government shutdown in history. President Donald Trump signed into law a federal spending package.

"The package passed by Congress includes three bills of full-year funding for specific departments, including agriculture and veteran affairs, but with most government agencies being funded through January 30. So, we could be on the verge of another shutdown in just over 10 weeks’ time, not the least if tensions over healthcare subsidies that Democrats had pushed for escalate between now and then," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Wall Street's major averages ended mixed on Wednesday, with the Dow Jones reaching a new record high close, while Nasdaq ended in the red, as several big tech stocks lagged.

With the shutdown now over for the time being, investors are hoping that delayed economic reports will soon start to come.

However, two major economic reports on jobs and inflation for October may never be released even as the government shutdown ends, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt has said.

"October consumer price inflation should have been published today. It is unlikely the number will ever be calculated, because there was no one to collect the price data. This highlights a problem —even if the U.S. government is functioning, there are fewer employees collecting prices, which means more of the inflation number is 'interpolated' (or 'guessed')," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

On the earnings side, Cisco (CSCO) shares rose 5.9% in early trade after the networking equipment giant reported fiscal first-quarter results that were above Wall Street's estimates and raised its full-year forecast.

The Walt Disney Company (DIS) fell 7.8% in early investor reaction to the company's FQ4 earnings report. Revenue dipped 0.5% year-over-year as the Entertainment segment saw revenue decline 6%.

On the economic side, there are more Federal Reserve speakers lined up on Thursday. This includes St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem and Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack.

The Fed's balance sheet will land later in the day.

More on markets

data.title: La Rosa secures $1.25B in financing amid repositioning toward data center infrastructure development
data.description:
La Rosa Holdings (LRHC) announced on Thursday a $1.25B financing, partly to fund its strategic repositioning toward the development of data center infrastructure for AI computing.

The company aims to repurpose and develop high-value properties into advanced data center facilities optimized for artificial intelligence workloads.

Shares were +15.73% pre-market to $4.93.

La Rosa has secured $1.25B in financing facilities from institutional investors, which includes a previously-announced $1B equity purchase facility and a $250M private placement convertible note facility.

The capital infusion is expected to enable the company to pursue strategic acquisitions and joint ventures.

A portion of initial proceeds is intended to be used to serve as a strategic reserve to support growth opportunities.

data.title: Short bets on S&P 500 Healthcare sector rises to 2.00% in October; MRNA stays most shorted stock
data.description:
The S&P 500 Healthcare sector (XLV) experienced a slight rise in average short interest during October, moving from 1.91% to 2.00%. Within this sector, Moderna (MRNA) remains the most heavily shorted stock.

The Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF (XLV), representing the healthcare sector and holding a 12.12% weight in the S&P 500, has seen an 11.09% year-to-date increase, lagging the broader S&P 500 index (SP500) which rose 16.48% during the same period.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

Even though short interest in Moderna (MRNA) remains the highest in the sector at 18.21% of its float, down from 18.66% last month, the company is garnering a strong vote of confidence from Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, which assigns a 4.02 out of 5 "Buy" score.

In late October 2025, Moderna's (MRNA) stock surged due to reports of talks with a large biopharma for a potential partnership or buyout provided a major boost to the stock, which had been trading at multi-year lows.

Recently, the company reported Q3 2025 financials that exceeded Wall Street forecasts, despite overall revenue declining sharply year-over-year due to waning COVID vaccine sales. Q3 2025 revenue was $1.0 Billion, beating the consensus, with $971 Million coming from COVID vaccine sales (mainly $781 Million from the U.S.).

The renewed investor optimism is driven by promising cancer vaccine data and surprising findings that the mRNA COVID vaccines may boost cancer survival rates in patients receiving immunotherapy. The company maintains a strong cash position and a commitment to reduced costs and a robust drug pipeline," a Seeking Alpha analyst wrote.

The second-most shorted stock in October was DaVita (NYSE:DVA) at 9.82%, while Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) was third at 8.44%.

On the other hand, several major companies had very low short interest, including Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) at 0.76%, Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) at 0.81%, STERIS plc (NYSE:STE) at 0.98%, and Merck & Co. (MRK) at 1.04%.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

For the nineteenth consecutive month, the biotechnology industry held the distinction of being the most shorted in the healthcare sector, with short interest rising to 2.97% from 2.86% in October. The healthcare provider and services sector followed with a short interest of 2.30% at the end of October, down from 2.33% in the prior month.

In contrast, the pharmaceuticals' industry recorded the lowest short interest among the healthcare sub-sectors in October, with a short percentage of 1.65% for the month, up from 1.53% in the prior month.

Related ETFs: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLV), SPDR S&P Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:XPH), VanEck Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), iShares Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBB), iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:IHE), and Invesco Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBBQ).

data.title: Sealed Air may be worth mid-$40s/share in takeover - analyst
data.description:
Sealed Air (SEE) may be worth mid-$40s/share in a takeover after reports on Tuesday that private equity firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice was in talks to take the packaging provider private, according to a Jefferies analyst. 

Shares of Sealed Air (SEE) jumped 21% in premarket trading to $44 on Thursday after the WSJ reported the takeover talks on Wednesday.

"We could see a take-out price in the $40-48/share range, lining up in the 8-9x EV/EBITDA range, not too far off from its historical trading multiple of 9-10x," Jefferies analyst Philip Ng, who has a hold rating and $40 price target, wrote in a note on Wednesday,
